name: Boone Bank & Trust Co.
description_short: Boone Bank & Trust Co. is a community bank serving Boone, Iowa since 1883, offering FDIC-insured deposit accounts, digital banking, loans, and business financial services.
description_long:
Boone Bank & Trust Co. has operated as a community-focused financial institution for over 140 years, originally founded as Boone County Bank in 1883. The bank has evolved through name changes and industry shifts while maintaining its core commitment to serving the Boone community and surrounding areas. This longevity demonstrates stability and deep local roots in a single geographic market.

The bank offers a range of traditional banking services including FDIC-insured checking and savings accounts, certificate of deposit (CDs), home equity lines of credit (HELOCs), personal and business loans, and credit card products. Their digital offerings include online banking, business digital banking, mobile app access, and tools specifically designed for business customers to manage payments, cash flow, and fraud protection. They also provide deposit rate information and support for various account types including their branded Relationship Rewards Checking product.

Boone Bank & Trust Co. distinguishes itself through its emphasis on community involvement and local decision-making. The bank actively publishes a Community Impact Report detailing support for local projects and organizations. They promote financial literacy through special offerings (such as complimentary custom debit cards for new users during Financial Literacy Month) and provide educational resources through banking insights articles. Their team-focused approach emphasizes getting to know individual bankers who serve the community.

As a regional community bank serving primarily the Boone, Iowa area, this institution is best suited for customers who value local banking relationships and community reinvestment. However, customers should note that geographic service limitations may restrict branch access compared to national banks, and product offerings may be more limited than larger financial institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificate of Deposit (CDs) with published deposit rates
Home Equity Lines of Credit (HELOCs) with limited-time specials
Business digital banking with payment and cash flow management tools
Fraud protection tools for business accounts
Financial literacy resources and educational articles
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of the U.S. Government
Over 140 years of continuous operation demonstrates financial stability and local commitment
Comprehensive digital banking tools including online banking, mobile app, and business digital banking
Community-focused with published Community Impact Report showing local reinvestment
Offers specialized business financial tools for payment management and cash flow
Financial literacy initiatives including complimentary debit cards for new users
Known routing number (073901097) and transparent banking operations
cons: Limited geographic footprint—primarily serves Boone, Iowa area, restricting branch access for non-local customers
Website does not provide detailed product specifications, rates, or loan terms for comparison
No information about minimum account balances, monthly fees, or account requirements
Limited mention of advanced digital features compared to larger national or neobanks
Phone-dependent customer service model (single number listed) may have limited hours
best_for: Boone, Iowa residents and business owners seeking stable, locally-rooted banking relationships
Small business owners needing personalized service and local decision-making authority
Customers prioritizing community reinvestment and local economic impact
Those seeking FDIC-insured traditional banking with established institutional history
diagnosis: Boone Bank & Trust Co. is best for Boone, Iowa-area customers and small business owners who value established community banking relationships, local decision-making, and FDIC protection. The main caveat is that this is a regional community bank with limited geographic reach—customers outside the Boone area will have restricted access to physical branches and may find more comprehensive digital offerings at larger national banks or neobanks.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Boone Bank & Trust Co. is a community bank serving Boone, Iowa since 1883, offering FDIC-insured accounts, digital banking, and loans.
